entities.name: LEGUMES

entities.name: NUTRIENT DEFICIENCY
entities.description: Lack of essential nutrients in the soil environment

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: Access to sufficient, safe, and nutritious food for poorest farmers

entities.name: NUTRIENT TOLERANCE
entities.description: Ability of a crop variety to perform well in low-nutrient environments

entities.name: LOW-NUTRIENT TOLERANT GENES
entities.description: Genes that confer tolerance to low-nutrient conditions

entities.name: CROP IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: Process of improving crop traits for better productivity and farmer acceptance

entities.name: FARMER PREFERENCES
entities.description: The priorities and desires of farmers regarding crop variety traits

entities.name: BELIEF SYSTEMS
entities.description: The set of beliefs held by farmers that influence technology adoption

entities.name: FARMER PARTICIPATORY RESEARCH
entities.description: Research approach involving farmers in the design and implementation of studies

entities.name: LEGUME-INTENSIFICATION
entities.description: Increasing the use or density of legumes in cropping systems

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY MANAGEMENT
entities.description: Practices to maintain or improve soil nutrient levels

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: Continent where legume-intensification research was reviewed

entities.name: MALAWI
entities.description: Country where on-farm trials and participatory research were conducted

entities.name: KENYA
entities.description: Country where doubled-up pigeonpea systems were studied

entities.name: INDETERMINATE GROWTH HABIT
entities.description: Growth pattern where the stem continues to grow, facilitating stress recovery

entities.name: LONG-DURATION
entities.description: Longer time from planting to maturity

entities.name: HIGH QUALITY RESIDUES
entities.description: Crop remains that provide significant organic matter and nutrients to the soil

entities.name: SHORT-DURATION
entities.description: Shorter time from planting to maturity

entities.name: DETERMINATE GROWTH HABIT
entities.description: Growth pattern where the stem stops growing after flowering

entities.name: NITROGEN FIXATION
entities.description: Biological process of converting atmospheric nitrogen into ammonia

entities.name: NUTRIENT ACQUISITION
entities.description: Ability of the plant to take up nutrients from the soil

entities.name: PHOTOSYNTHESIS
entities.description: Process of converting light energy to chemical energy

entities.name: GRAIN FILL
entities.description: Stage of seed development and accumulation of reserves

entities.name: INTERMITTENT STRESSES
entities.description: Occasional environmental stressors such as drought or pests

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: Lack of water availability in the environment

entities.name: PEST PRESSURE
entities.description: Damage and stress caused by agricultural pests

entities.name: LATE MATURITY
entities.description: Delayed time to reach maturity, often associated with indeterminate growth

entities.name: MODERATE YIELD POTENTIAL
entities.description: Average capacity to produce yield

entities.name: HIGH LABOUR DEMAND
entities.description: Requirement for significant human effort for crop management

entities.name: EARLY MATURITY
entities.description: Fast time to reach maturity, prioritized by women farmers

entities.name: LOW-LABOUR REQUIREMENT
entities.description: Requirement for minimal human effort, prioritized by women farmers

entities.name: YIELD POTENTIAL
entities.description: Maximum possible yield under optimal conditions

entities.description: A legume crop interplanted with pigeonpea

entities.name: CALORIE PRODUCTION
entities.description: Total energy produced per unit area in intensified systems

entities.name: DOUBLED-UP PIGEONPEA SYSTEM
entities.description: A specific intercropping system with double rows of pigeonpea and maize

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A cereal crop intercropped with pigeonpea in doubled-up systems

entities.name: INTERCROP SUITABILITY
entities.description: Ability of a variety to perform well when grown with other crops

entities.name: REDUCED EARLY BRANCHING
entities.description: A specific trait that improves the intercrop suitability of pigeonpea

entities.name: ON-FARM TRIALS
